everyone.name: Nina Polshakova
everyone.bio: Nina is a principal software engineer working on multi-cluster Istio solutions and API Gateways at Solo.io. She has been on the release team for Kubernetes v1.27-v1.31, in roles including the Enhancements team lead and the Release Notes lead. Previously Nina worked at Shape Security preventing malicious automation attacks 🤖.

everyone.name: Irvi Firqotul Aini
everyone.bio: Interested in open source, machine learning, compiler, and natural language processing. Exploring language parsing with Rust and R. Part of Shadow of Kubernetes Release Team 1.17 and 1.18. Shadow Chairs of Kubernetes SIGs Docs. Emeritus-Co-Chairs of Kubernetes SIGs Docs. Contributing in Kubernetes, Planout and Ax (Facebook), Google Cloud Platform and Google API (Google), Keras. Programme Committee at Helm Summit 2019. Programme Committee at Grace Hopper 2020. Programme Committee at KubeCon + CloudNativeCon APAC 2020. Programme Committee at KubeCon + CloudNativeCon NA 2020. KubeCon and CloudNativeCon Track Chair for ML & Data EU 2021 NA 2022. Track Host for CPPCon 2022. Mentor At Grace Hopper OSD 2022.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm thrilled to be a Cloud Native Foundation ambassador because it offers an incredible platform to advocate for state-of-the-art cloud technologies and best practices. Being part of this community allows me to engage with thought leaders, share knowledge, and contribute to shaping the future of cloud computing. Moreover, this role empowers me to bridge the gap between developers, businesses, and the broader public, fostering innovation and growth in the ecosystem.

everyone.name: Joe Searcy
everyone.bio: Joe is a Staff Software Engineer. He's a passionate technologist and loves solving complex problems. Joe leads the Kubernetes Atlanta Meetup and is a contributor to many CNCF projects (Kubernetes, Istio, Carvel, etc.). Outside of technology, Joe is an audiophile, a lego enthusiast, and a practitioner of Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu.

everyone.name: Tipsukanya Norrasing
everyone.bio: Starting my journey as a Chinese language arts student, I am always grateful for the opportunities to learn and grow in the tech industry since my university days. From gradually teaching myself to understand the world of Cloud Native and Kubernetes, I am incredibly happy that these small steps have led me to achieve the Kubestronaut title. I still see myself as a lifelong learner who enjoys exploring DevOps and DevSecOps every day.

everyone.name: Mingming Tao
everyone.bio: 20+ years of experience in financial and IT industries, specializing in production support for mainframe and  cloud-native systems. Drive performance, reliability, and operability through continuous improvement. Author of the Ten Thousand Whys of SRE column. Passionate about dissecting complex issues and advancing operational excellence. Always curious, hands‑on, and eager to solve hard problems.

everyone.name: Patrick Eichler
everyone.bio: I'm Patrick, a freelancer who specializes in site reliability engineering and Kubernetes. Through reading IT books, IT-Bloqs, and autodidactic studies, I constantly broaden my horizon with state-of-the-art expert knowledge and modern technical skills, which I not only use in my daily work and personal projects, but also blog about and teach to other people.

ambassadors.name: Nina Polshakova
ambassadors.bio: Nina is a principal software engineer working on multi-cluster Istio solutions and API Gateways at Solo.io. She has been on the release team for Kubernetes v1.27-v1.31, in roles including the Enhancements team lead and the Release Notes lead. Previously Nina worked at Shape Security preventing malicious automation attacks 🤖.

ambassadors.name: Mélony Qin
ambassadors.bio: I'm passionate about open-source and cloud-native technologies, particularly Kubernetes and serverless, and their intersection with Data & AI workloads. I authored multiple books about Kubernetes over the years with the intention to help learners skill up on Kubernetes and find a new career path. I'm proud and excited to be a CNCF ambassador! Being an ambassador allows me to connect more with the community. I would love to share knowledge and learn, and I hope to help others learn about cloud-native technologies like Kubernetes and how Kubernetes serves as the backbone in the age of AI. I will continue to create engaging educational content to help people navigate the complexities of Kubernetes adoption more effectively. I am sharing tech educational content on my YouTube channel: https://www.youtube.com/@CloudMelonVis and on my blog: https://cvisiona.com. And last but not least my favorite Github : https://github.com/cloudmelon, I'm happy to connect!

ambassadors.name: Fabrizio Lazzaretti
ambassadors.bio: Fabrizio Lazzaretti is a Managing Consultant specializing in software architecture at Wavestone. He has over a decade of expertise in software architecture, development, and DevOps. He is a maintainer of the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) project CloudEvents. Recognized for his proficiency as both a team player and leader in software development, architecture, and Scrum, he excels in dynamic work settings. His primary areas of expertise lie in event-driven architecture and microservice design within cloud environments. Currently, he is actively engaged in enterprise architecture within the insurance sector.

ambassadors.name: Vyom Yadav
ambassadors.bio: Vyom is a final-year computer science student who has been working with open source throughout his degree program. He has been awarded the Google OSPB Award for contributing to Kubernetes. He has been a contributor to Kubernetes, Kyverno and KubeArmor. He co-organizes the CNCF Chandigarh Chapter.

ambassadors.name: Tiffany Jernigan
ambassadors.bio: Tiffany is a CNCF Ambassador, seasoned technologist and content creator in the Cloud Native space. She most recently was a senior developer advocate at VMware. She also formerly worked as a software developer and developer advocate at Amazon, Docker, and Intel. Prior to that, she graduated from Georgia Tech with a degree in electrical engineering. You can find her at tiffanyfay.dev (and for Bluesky).

ambassadors.name: 子篆 林
ambassadors.bio: My current focus is cloud-native computing, programming languages, and system software. I'm working as a compiler developer at secondstate. My blog https://dannypsnl.me writes technical content about formal methods and mathematics. I'm an open source technology enthusiast, and very excited to push formal methods in cloud computing.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm passionate about helping people as I could, been an ambassador is a well way to do so. Besides that, this is a good opportunities do learn from others, like how to maintain communities, or their invaluable personal perspectives.

ambassadors.name: Mathieu Benoit
ambassadors.bio: I'm passionate about Cloud Native Computing technologies driven by Cloud, Security, Kubernetes and Platform Engineering. I'm one of the co-organizers of the CNCF&K8s Meetup in Quebec city (Canada). You will find some of my learnings in my personal blog: https://mathieu-benoit.github.io/ and in my Medium space: https://medium.com/@mabenoit.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Being a Cloud Native Ambassador will allow me to be closer to the CNCF community, and be connected to more mentors to grow my knowledge and as an individual too. It will also allow me to find more tips to promote content related to CNCF projects, and hopefully I will be able to help and inspire others to be more involved in this community.

ambassadors.name: Jacopo Nardiello
ambassadors.bio: Founder at SIGHUP, I have been involved with Kubernetes and the CNCF since its inception in 2015. I am passionate about Kubernetes and full lifecycle management of production-grade infrastructures, open standards and currently serving in the advisory board of Linux Foundation Europe.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm thrilled to continue supporting the CNCF, both as a community of like-minded individuals and as a technological ecosystem. My mission is to encourage organizations to adopt open source projects as a foundation for the evolution of their infrastructure and governance, promoting the use of OSS and Open Standards.

kubestronauts.name: Tipsukanya Norrasing
kubestronauts.bio: Starting my journey as a Chinese language arts student, I am always grateful for the opportunities to learn and grow in the tech industry since my university days. From gradually teaching myself to understand the world of Cloud Native and Kubernetes, I am incredibly happy that these small steps have led me to achieve the Kubestronaut title. I still see myself as a lifelong learner who enjoys exploring DevOps and DevSecOps every day.

kubestronauts.name: Labhesh Hase
kubestronauts.bio: I'm Labhesh Hase, a Kubestronaut passionate about cloud-native security and DevSecOps. I specialize in OCI, AWS, Kubernetes, Terraform, and CNCF/cloud security tools like Kyverno, CIS Benchmarks, and Trivy. I help customers across the globe migrate from on-prem to cloud by designing and architecting secure, scalable cloud platforms. I also enjoy long-distance running, using it as a way to stay mentally focused while balancing the intense demands of tech and continuous learning.

kubestronauts.name: Bolivar David Llerena Fuenmayor
kubestronauts.bio: An experienced tech consultant with over 8 years of expertise in software development, cloud computing, and machine learning. Bolivar has worked with multinationals such as McKinsey & Company, Kyndryl, and IBM, and holds over 400 certifications across AWS, Azure, GCP, and Cisco. He was honored with the José Gualberto Pérez Award for excellence in physics and mathematics and is passionate about continuous learning.

kubestronauts.name: Mohan Kandasamy
kubestronauts.bio: I am IT consultant with over 20 years of experience in UNIX/Linux space and recently started working on Cloud Native technologies.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Zekiye Aydemir
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I am a PostgreSQL&MongoDB and Kubernetes specialist with 7 years of experience managing large-scale, mission-critical systems. My expertise spans database optimization, cluster security, and cloud-native solutions. I’ve contributed to open-source initiatives like CloudNativePG and enjoy working on complex technical challenges in containerized environments. Passionate about continuous learning, I actively engage with the Kubernetes ecosystem and thrive in international teams, sharing knowledge and collaborating on innovative solutions.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Chris Jaimon
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Code whisperer by day, YAML exorcist by night — this developer lives and breathes Kubernetes, occasionally dreaming in Helm charts. As a die-hard fan of all things Cloud Native, I believe containers are the new black and "kubectl apply" is a lifestyle choice. From fine-tuning autoscalers with Karpenter to taming sidecars with Istio, I treat Cloud Native like an art form. When not debugging pods, I'm probably evangelizing service meshes to unsuspecting coffee shop patrons :)
